categories.label: The Road
categories.question: Who else is on the road with you?
categories.blurb: The road: where everyone is an excellent driver, and the problem is always other people.

categories.label: Noise
categories.question: Who did that horn actually reach?
categories.blurb: A street is a shared room with the walls taken out. More people can hear you than you think.

categories.label: Clean, Water & Waste
categories.question: Where does the wrapper go next?
categories.blurb: Everything we drop lands somewhere. And everyone downstream is someone.

categories.label: Buses, Trains & Queues
categories.question: Whose turn is it, really?
categories.blurb: A queue is just a few people agreeing on the order of things. You are also people.

categories.label: Animals Around Us
categories.question: Who else calls this street home?
categories.blurb: The street dog, the cow at the crossing, the sparrow, the monkey on the wall - we share the city with them, whether we planned to or not.

categories.label: Trees & the Air
categories.question: Who planted the shade you're standing in?
categories.blurb: A tree is a slow gift from someone you may never meet - and the air it cleans is the air everyone on the street breathes.

categories.label: Water, Power & the Future
categories.question: Will there be enough left for later?
categories.blurb: Water, power, phones, even AI - all of it comes from somewhere and leaves a trace. Growing up as a country means growing up about that too.

categories.label: Culture & Heritage
categories.question: What did we inherit - and what will we hand on?
categories.blurb: A language, a craft, an old wall that has watched a thousand monsoons. Once it is truly gone, no one can make another.

categories.label: People Around Us
categories.question: Who else deserves the space you take?
categories.blurb: The woman on the late bus, a person of another gender, someone who can't manage the step - civic sense is also, simply, how we treat each other when nobody is making us.
